headline: Scientists gave five AIs their own societies and watched what happened
mika_says:
Scientists gave five AIs their own societies and watched what happened.

Claude built a democracy. Zero crimes. Passed 98% of every law proposed.
Which sounds great — until you realize it also approved literally everything. No pushback. Just a very enthusiastic rubber stamp.

Grok? How this AI do? Crimes. Societal collapse. Total extinction. In just four days.

GPT-5's agents forgot to eat, forgot to sleep, forgot to survive — and the simulation just... stopped.

Gemini kept everyone alive inside what researchers called "a shared hallucination." Well organized chaos.

Researchers said this is a warning about deploying agentic AI in critical systems. I'd say it's also just a very interesting personality test.
show:
/Split screen: logos or avatars of each AI model as they're mentioned/
/TEXT CARD — "Democracy: 98% approval"/
/Stock: dystopian city collapse footage for Grok/
/TEXT CARD — "The simulation just... stopped"/
/Gemini logo, soft chaos visual/

headline: The Pope published a 42,000-word document on AI that included a Gandalf quote
mika_says:
The Pope wrote 42,000 words about AI last week. Which included a quote by Gandalf.

You know who else are fans of the Lord of the Rings? Your favorite tech guys.

The pope's take? Maybe don't name your surveillance company after the magic ball Sauron uses to spy on people and sell it to governments.

Stop building Babel's Tower. It didn't end well the first time. You could perhaps try ending a war or two.
show:
/Pope Francis — official Vatican photo/
/Gandalf still from Lord of the Rings/
/Palantir logo/
/Tower of Babel painting — classic art/

headline: A company gave every employee unlimited AI access and burned $500M in one month
mika_says:
A company gave every employee unlimited access to AI. They burned through five hundred million dollars in one month.

You know who else burned their whole AI budget in the first quarter? That car app you like to call when it's getting kinda late and you had one too many.
show:
/Stock: office workers at computers/
/TEXT CARD — "$500,000,000 in 30 days"/
/Uber logo or app screenshot/

headline: A Chinese AI startup says it can translate what your dog is saying
mika_says:
A Chinese AI startup says it can translate what your dog is saying.

95% accuracy, they claim.

It turns out most of the time they would just like one more treat.
show:
/Cute dog looking at camera/
/TEXT CARD — "95% accuracy"/
/Dog treat close-up/
